transcript.text: I can’t even get my graphic novel started, and my parents are still on my case like a bad sequel. I feel like nobody’s listening, and the thought of the school event makes me want to disappear. I just want to be left alone.

The pressure from your parents and the school event is really getting to you, and it's making you feel like you just want to disappear. It sounds like you're feeling really overwhelmed by the deadline and your self-doubt about your drawing abilities is kicking in. I can see why you'd feel like nobody's listening, especially when it feels like everyone's expectations are weighing on you. Yeah, it's a lot to handle, and it's okay to feel that way.

I guess I'm just stuck. My parents keep telling me to finish that comic, but I'm too tired to even think about it. And the event? I don't even want to think about showing anything. I'm just so behind and nobody even cares.

You're feeling exhausted, and the thought of working on your graphic novel is just too much right now. It's like the pressure from your parents and the upcoming event is suffocating you, and you just want to escape. I can see why you'd feel that way, especially when it feels like nobody's listening to your concerns. Yeah, it's okay to feel stuck and overwhelmed. Can you tell me more about what's been going on with your parents and how that's affecting your motivation to work on your comic?

Yeah, they’re just yelling about deadlines again. I feel like they think my comic is a joke and that I’m just procrastinating on purpose. I don’t even want to talk about it, it makes me want to disappear.

It's really tough to feel like your parents think your comic is a joke, and that they're not taking your struggles seriously. It sounds like their pressure is making you feel even more anxious about your drawing abilities, and like you're just not good enough. I can see why you'd want to disappear when it feels like nobody's listening to your concerns. Yeah, it's okay to feel that way. Can you tell me more about what you think would need to happen for you to feel like you can start working on your graphic novel again?

If they just stopped yelling and let me have a quiet corner for a bit, maybe I'd actually get something done. Right now, every time I try to think about the comic, they’re back with deadlines and I just freeze. I’m not even sure I can focus if they’re always on my case.

Having a quiet corner to yourself might really help you get started on your graphic novel. It sounds like the constant yelling and deadline reminders from your parents are really throwing you off and making it hard to focus. If you had that quiet space, do you think you'd be able to break through some of that anxiety and get into your project?
I don’t even know if a quiet corner can help. My parents are still yelling, and I just feel like I can’t focus or talk about it.

The yelling from your parents is still really affecting you, and it's making it tough to even think about your comic. You've mentioned wanting a quiet corner to work on it, but it's hard to focus when they're always bringing up deadlines. Yeah, it can be really frustrating when it feels like nobody's listening to your concerns. What do you think would make it possible for you to start working on your comic again, even if it's just a small step?

I keep putting the comic off because every time I try, the parents start shouting about deadlines. Even if I had a quiet corner, the yelling would still echo in my head. I don't want to show anything at the event; I just want to disappear. I feel so alone, like nobody gets me.

The yelling from your parents is still echoing in your head, and it's making it really tough to focus on your comic. It's like every time you try to start, you're immediately brought back to those deadline reminders and the feeling of being overwhelmed. You're feeling so alone right now, like nobody understands what you're going through. Yeah, it can be really frustrating when it feels like your parents aren't listening to your concerns.

Honestly, I'm just stuck in this loop. Every time I try to focus on the comic, the yelling is like a soundtrack I can't turn off. I keep putting it off because I know the event is coming and I can't even face the crowd. I feel so alone and like no one gets it.

The yelling from your parents is still really getting to you, and it's like it's always playing in the background, making it hard to focus on your comic. It's understandable that you're feeling overwhelmed and alone, especially with the event coming up and the pressure to perform. It sounds like you're feeling really trapped in this situation, and I can see why you'd want to escape. Yeah, it's okay to feel that way. What's the smallest thing you think you could do right now to start feeling a bit more in control of your comic, even if it's just a tiny step?

Yeah, maybe I could doodle for a minute, but the yelling still feels like a bad soundtrack and I’m not sure that helps. The whole thing feels like a loop and I’m not even looking forward to the event. Honestly, I just want to disappear.

The yelling from your parents is still really affecting you, and it's making it tough to even think about your comic, let alone doodle. It's like that soundtrack of criticism is always playing in the background, making you feel like you're not good enough. I can see why you'd feel like you're stuck in a loop and just want to disappear. It takes a lot of courage to admit that, and I'm here to support you. Yeah, it's okay to feel that way. What do you think is the one thing that would make you feel slightly more in control of your situation right now?
I don't know what would help, but if they just left me alone for a few minutes, maybe I could at least finish a page. The whole thing feels like a loop and I'm stuck.

You're feeling really overwhelmed and just want some space to focus on your comic without the constant yelling and deadline reminders from your parents. It's like every time you try to start, you're immediately brought back to those stressful feelings. Maybe taking a few minutes to yourself, without any distractions, could help you clear your head and make some progress on your page. What do you think you could do in that alone time to help you feel more grounded and focused?
I could try doodling a quick sketch, but the noise from them still makes it hard to even start. If I get a few minutes alone, maybe I can at least finish a page, but I'm not sure it will stick.

The noise from your parents is still making it hard for you to start doodling, and it's understandable that you're unsure if it will stick. Having a few minutes alone might help you focus on your comic, and doodling could be a good way to begin. What if you try setting a timer for just a few minutes, and during that time, you focus on doodling without any distractions? Sometimes, taking small steps like that can help us feel more in control and build momentum towards our goals.

turn_traces.user_message: I can’t even get my graphic novel started, and my parents are still on my case like a bad sequel. I feel like nobody’s listening, and the thought of the school event makes me want to disappear. I just want to be left alone.
